title: Best Buttermilk Biscuit Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups all-purpose flour (can substitute 1:1 GF flour)
7 tablespoons unsalted butter, chilled in freezer and cut into thin slices
instructions: Cut in butter until crumbly mixture using pastry blender or your hands.
Lightly stir in cold buttermilk until forms a dough.
Place on flour covered board and form into a rectangle.
Turn dough a half turn, gather any crumbs, and flatten back into a rectangle.
Repeat folding and pressing the dough two more times for a total of three folds.
If you have time, refrigerate the dough for 30 minutes or more.
Roll out the dough in a rectangle about ½ inch thick.
Cut into biscuits, making a dozen.
Place biscuits on parchment lined baking sheet.
Brush tops with buttermilk and bake at 400 degrees F for 15-16 minutes.
notes: All-purpose flour can be substituted 1:1 with gluten-free flour
Butter should be chilled in freezer and cut into thin slices for best results
Dough can be refrigerated for 30 minutes or more before rolling out
